name: BridgeNode Tools for Claude
description: Tool definitions for Claude to interact with BridgeNode server

tools.name: write_to_bn_output
tools.description: Push content to BridgeNode frontend via SSE/WebSocket. Use this tool when you need to output long code blocks, provide results for users to copy, or send structured data that should be displayed in the frontend UI. This is the REQUIRED method for delivering output - DO NOT just print to console.

content.description: The content to push to the frontend. Can be plain text, markdown, code blocks, or structured data.

label.description: Optional label to categorize the output (e.g., 'Code Result', 'Analysis', 'Debug Info'). Default: 'Claude Output'

examples.description: Push a code result to frontend

params.content: Analysis complete. Found 42 items matching criteria.
params.label: Analysis

usage_notes: ALWAYS use this tool when outputting code, long text, or any content that needs to be displayed in the frontend
The content will be broadcast via SSE and WebSocket to all connected frontend clients
Each output gets a unique ID and timestamp for tracking
Code blocks will be automatically extracted and made available for one-click copying

tools.name: fetch_context_by_id
tools.description: Retrieve stored context data by its unique ID (UUID). Use this when a user provides a context UUID or ID - you MUST call this tool to fetch the actual content. The context can contain documents, code, or any stored data that needs to be retrieved.

data_id.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the context to retrieve. This is typically provided by the user in the format 'uuid:xxxx' or just the UUID string.

usage_notes: When user provides a UUID or mentions 'context', you MUST call this tool to retrieve the content
The system maintains a context registry that maps IDs to actual stored data
Returns both metadata and the actual content of the context
If context not found, returns error with available context IDs for reference

tools.name: list_available_contexts
tools.description: List all available context IDs in the registry. Use this to discover what contexts are available when the user doesn't provide a specific ID.

limit.description: Maximum number of contexts to return

workflows.output_delivery: 1. Generate content -> 2. Call write_to_bn_output -> 3. Frontend receives via SSE/WebSocket -> 4. User can copy from UI
workflows.context_retrieval: 1. User provides UUID -> 2. Call fetch_context_by_id -> 3. Process retrieved content -> 4. Generate response
